title: Conformance Test Suite with JSON Test Vectors

artifacts.description: JSON Schema (draft-07) defining the test vector format: circuit name enum, input signals object, expected_output object or expected_rejection string, category tag, and optional notes field
Master file containing 50+ test vectors grouped by category covering IdentityHandshake, DelegationLink, and ScopeEnforcement circuits
Explains vector format, category taxonomy, how to run the suite against any snarkjs-compatible implementation, and expected pass/fail counts per category
Node.js script that validates vectors.json against vector-schema.json using ajv; exits non-zero on any schema violation
Standalone runner that loads vectors.json, calls snarkjs groth16.fullProve or expects proof rejection, and reports pass/fail per vector without depending on any project-specific test harness

steps: Define the JSON Schema for a single test vector: required fields are 'id' (string), 'circuit' (enum: IdentityHandshake | DelegationLink | ScopeEnforcement), 'category' (enum: valid_handshake | expired_credential | revoked_nullifier | stale_merkle_root | scope_subset_violation | cumulative_bit_violation | delegation_depth | cross_hop_scope_mismatch), 'inputs' (object of signal-name → value), and 'expected' (object with 'outputs' for valid cases or 'rejection_reason' string for invalid cases)

Enumerate all signal names for each of the three circuits from the existing circuit specs; create a reference table mapping circuit → input signals → output signals to ensure vectors use correct field names

Generate category 'valid_handshake': 8 vectors covering human-only credential, agent-delegated credential at depth 1, agent at depth 3, varying scope bitmasks (single scope, multi-scope), and boundary timestamp (currentTimestamp == expiryTimestamp - 1)
Generate category 'expired_credential': 6 vectors where currentTimestamp >= expiryTimestamp for both human and agent credentials; expected_rejection_reason = 'ExpiryCheckFailed'
Generate category 'revoked_nullifier': 5 vectors where the human nullifier hash appears in the revocation Merkle tree; expected_rejection_reason = 'NullifierRevoked'
Generate category 'stale_merkle_root': 5 vectors where the presented Merkle root is not in the 30-root rolling buffer; expected_rejection_reason = 'StaleRoot'

Generate category 'scope_subset_violation': 6 vectors where the agent's requested scope bits include at least one bit not set in the delegating human's scope; expected_rejection_reason = 'ScopeNotSubset'

Generate category 'cumulative_bit_violation': 6 vectors testing the dependency rule that bit 4 requires bits 2 and 3 both set; include cases where only bit 4 is set, bit 4 + bit 2 only, bit 4 + bit 3 only, and valid case where bits 2+3+4 are all set

Generate category 'delegation_depth': 8 vectors: depth 0 (human direct), depth 1 (single agent hop), depth 3 (max allowed), depth 4 (must fail with 'MaxDelegationDepthExceeded'), plus two edge cases with mixed valid/invalid intermediate hops

Generate category 'cross_hop_scope_commitment_mismatch': 6 vectors where the scope commitment hash at hop N does not match the re-derived commitment at hop N+1 in the delegation chain; expected_rejection_reason = 'ScopeCommitmentMismatch'
Assign deterministic numeric IDs and stable UUIDs to each vector so implementations can reference failures by ID in bug reports
Write validate-schema.js using ajv to confirm every vector in vectors.json satisfies vector-schema.json; run locally to confirm zero errors before committing

Write run-suite.js as a standalone executor: for 'outputs' vectors call groth16.fullProve and assert signal values match; for 'rejection_reason' vectors expect proof generation to throw or constraint check to fail and match the reason string
Add a summary block at the top of vectors.json with total count, per-category count, and per-circuit count for quick human verification
Write conformance/README.md documenting the schema, category definitions, how to point the runner at custom wasm/zkey artifacts, and how to interpret the pass/fail report
dependencies: Finalized signal names for IdentityHandshake circuit
Finalized signal names for DelegationLink circuit
Finalized signal names for ScopeEnforcement circuit
Specification of the 30-root rolling buffer interface
Specification of the revocation Merkle tree structure
Agreed cumulative bit dependency rules (bit 4 requires bits 2+3)
ajv npm package for schema validation runner
snarkjs npm package for proof generation in the standalone runner
